title: Spacelabs Healthcare UltraView SL Series - Power-On / Boot Failure
description: Guide to troubleshoot UltraView SL Series patient monitor failing to power on or complete boot sequence

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Never troubleshoot the monitor while it is connected to an active patient. Move the patient to a functional monitor if necessary.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Check that the monitor is plugged into a live outlet. Confirm the outlet is working by testing with another device.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions: Examine the power cord for visible damage, kinks, or fraying. Ensure the cord is fully seated into both the monitor and wall outlet.

steps.title: Check Battery Status (if applicable)
steps.instructions: If the unit has an internal battery, verify it is charged or try powering the monitor with AC only. Swap with a known good battery if available.

steps.title: Perform a Hard Reset / Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Disconnect the monitor from AC power and remove the battery (if removable). Wait 30–60 seconds, then reconnect AC and battery. Attempt to power on the monitor.

steps.title: Observe Indicator Lights or Audible Alarms
steps.instructions: Note any LED patterns, beeps, or error messages. Record these exactly, as they can indicate specific faults.

steps.title: Verify Peripherals and Network Cables
steps.instructions: Disconnect all non-essential peripherals, USB drives, and network cables. Attempt to boot with minimal connections.

steps.title: Check for Recent Firmware or Configuration Changes
steps.instructions: Confirm with staff if any updates were applied prior to failure.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor will not power on; display remains blank when plugged in.
CCR.Cause: Power cord and outlet verified; battery checked; hard reset performed; monitor still fails to boot.
CCR.Resolution: Monitor removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Power cord inspected and swapped if necessary
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Spacelabs Healthcare UltraView SL Series - ECG Lead Connection / Module Errors
description: Troubleshooting guide for ECG lead connection issues and module errors on UltraView SL Series monitors.

steps.title: Confirm Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Ensure the patient is on a stable monitor or other device before troubleshooting. Do not attempt ECG troubleshooting on an actively unstable patient.

steps.title: Verify Power and Monitor Status
steps.instructions: Check that the monitor is fully powered on and has completed boot. Confirm no startup errors are present that could affect module detection.

steps.title: Inspect ECG Cables and Connectors
steps.instructions: Examine the ECG lead wires for fraying, cracks, or bent pins. Ensure connectors are fully seated in both the module and patient electrodes. Swap with a known good cable if available to rule out cable faults.

steps.title: Check Electrode Placement and Skin Contact
steps.instructions: Confirm electrodes are properly placed and have adequate skin contact. Replace dried-out or poorly adhering electrodes. Clean skin if necessary to reduce impedance.

steps.title: Verify ECG Module Seating
steps.instructions: Check that the ECG module (if removable) is fully seated in the monitor. Listen for a click or visual confirmation on the monitor display.

steps.title: Observe Monitor Behavior
steps.instructions: After reconnecting leads and module, observe for lead detection messages. Verify waveforms for stability and noise reduction.

steps.title: Check for Environmental or Electrical Interference
steps.instructions: Ensure leads are not running near strong electrical equipment. Move the monitor if necessary to minimize 60 Hz interference or EMI.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor intermittently shows ‘Lead Off’ and ECG waveform drops out.
CCR.Cause: Leads tested; cable was damaged; electrode placement inconsistent. Module seated properly; issue persists with known-good cables.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for repair. Patient monitored on backup device during evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Outlet and power supply status verified
All ECG cables inspected or swapped
Monitor display for any error codes or messages

title: Spacelabs Healthcare UltraView SL Series - NIBP / SpO₂ Sensor Measurement Failures
description: Troubleshooting guide for UltraView SL Series patient monitor when NIBP or SpO₂ sensor measurements fail, emphasizing external checks before module failure.

steps.title: Check Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Ensure the patient is not dependent on the monitor for immediate therapy. Move patient to another monitor if needed. Only troubleshoot when patient is safe.

steps.title: Verify Power and Basic Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm the monitor is powered on and fully booted. Check battery or AC connection. A low battery can cause sensor measurement errors.

steps.title: Inspect NIBP Cuff and Tubing
steps.instructions: Ensure the cuff is appropriate size and properly attached to the patient. Check for kinks, leaks, or disconnected tubing. Swap with a known good cuff to rule out cuff defect.

steps.title: Inspect SpO₂ Sensor and Cable
steps.instructions: Confirm correct sensor type for the UltraView SL. Check sensor placement on patient (finger, toe, or earlobe). Examine cable for damage, loose connections, or corrosion at connectors. Try a known working sensor on the same port to isolate sensor vs. module issue.

steps.title: Check Monitor Ports and Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect the sensor ports for debris, bent pins, or loose connections. Re-seat the sensor connectors firmly.

steps.title: Verify Configuration Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm the monitor is configured for the correct NIBP and SpO₂ measurement type. Ensure alarm limits are appropriate (overly restrictive limits can trigger measurement errors).

steps.title: Test with a Known Good Patient Simulator (Optional)
steps.instructions: If available, connect a patient simulator to confirm the monitor reads NIBP and SpO₂ correctly. Helps distinguish between sensor failure and monitor module failure.

CCR.Complaint: NIBP and SpO₂ measurements are failing intermittently; alarms indicate sensor error.
CCR.Cause: Observed that the SpO₂ sensor cable was loose and the NIBP cuff had a small leak; re-tested sensors with known-good units.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected sensors and replaced faulty NIBP cuff. Confirmed measurements stable. Device returned to service.

title: Spacelabs Healthcare UltraView SL Series - Alarm Configuration Troubleshooting
description: Guide for Clinical Engineering to troubleshoot UltraView SL Series patient monitors when alarms are silent, not triggering, or misconfigured.

steps.title: Verify Power and Boot Status
steps.instructions: Ensure the monitor is fully powered on. Check for any startup errors or “System Error” messages. A partially booted system may prevent alarms from functioning.

steps.title: Check Alarm Settings
steps.instructions: Access the alarm configuration menu and confirm limits are set correctly for each parameter (HR, SpO₂, NIBP, etc.). Ensure alarms are enabled for each parameter. Verify alarm volume is set above minimum and not muted.

steps.title: Inspect External Connections
steps.instructions: Confirm all patient cables (ECG leads, SpO₂ sensor, NIBP cuff) are securely connected. Some alarm triggers depend on valid sensor input; disconnected or faulty sensors may prevent alarms from triggering.

steps.title: Check Audio and Visual Indicators
steps.instructions: Ensure the monitor’s speaker is unobstructed and functional. Verify that visual alarm indicators (lights, screen prompts) illuminate appropriately. Swap the speaker or display with a known working unit if available.

steps.title: Review Recent Configuration Changes
steps.instructions: Check for recent software updates, user profile changes, or reset events that may have altered alarm settings. Restore default alarm settings temporarily to verify if the alarm triggers correctly.

steps.title: Test with Simulated Parameters
steps.instructions: If safe, use a test simulator or change patient parameters (offline or with test mode) to exceed alarm limits. Observe whether alarms sound and visual indicators respond.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor alarms are not triggering when patient parameters exceed limits; some alarms are silent.
CCR.Cause: External checks showed alarms disabled or volume muted; all patient connections verified, power confirmed.
CCR.Resolution: Restored alarm settings to defaults, verified speaker functionality. If alarms still did not trigger, removed device from service and labeled Out of Service for repair.

helpfulDetails: Alarm behavior: Silent for high/low limits
Indicator lights: No visual alerts during simulated test
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Spacelabs Healthcare UltraView SL Series - Network / EMR / Central Station Communication Failure
description: Troubleshooting guide for UltraView SL Series patient monitor communication issues with EMR or central station.

steps.title: Confirm Network Connection
steps.instructions: Check the network cable is securely connected to the monitor and wall port. Verify the port is active by testing with a known-good device. Why: Loose or inactive connections are the most common cause of communication failures.

steps.title: Verify IP and Network Settings
steps.instructions: Access the monitor’s network settings via the menu. Confirm the IP address, subnet mask, gateway, and DNS are correct for your network. Ensure no IP conflicts exist with other devices. Why: Incorrect network configuration prevents communication with EMR and central station.

steps.title: Check EMR / Central Station Status
steps.instructions: Verify the central station or EMR server is online and accepting connections. Confirm the monitor’s identifier or hostname is registered on the network. Why: Communication failure can originate from the receiving system rather than the monitor.

steps.title: Restart Monitor and Network Devices
steps.instructions: Power-cycle the monitor. If possible, restart network switches or access points connected to the monitor. Why: Temporary network or software glitches can resolve with a simple reboot.

steps.title: Verify Firewall or Security Restrictions
steps.instructions: Confirm hospital network security settings allow traffic from the monitor. Ensure required ports for Spacelabs communication are open. Why: Security policies can block data transfer even if the network cable is connected.

steps.title: Test Alternate Network Path
steps.instructions: Connect the monitor to a different network port known to be working. If possible, test on a different subnet that has EMR access. Why: Helps isolate whether the issue is port, switch, or monitor-specific.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor not sending patient data to EMR or appearing on central station.
CCR.Cause: Network cable disconnected and monitor IP settings were incorrect; central station online and functional.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected cable, verified correct IP configuration, monitor successfully communicated with EMR.

helpfulDetails: Network port tested and confirmed active
Cable swapped with known working cable
Indicator lights on network port (link/activity) normal
Final device status: functional on network

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Qube - Network / EMR / Central Station Communication Failure
description: Guide for troubleshooting Spacelabs Qube patient monitors that fail to communicate with EMR, central station, or network.

steps.title: Verify Physical Network Connections
steps.instructions: Check that the Ethernet cable is fully seated in both the monitor and network port. Look for damaged or loose cables. Test the same cable with a known working monitor to rule out cable failure.

steps.title: Check Network Indicators
steps.instructions: Verify the monitor’s network LED lights (if present) for activity and link status. Ensure the network switch port shows a connection.

steps.title: Confirm IP Address / Network Settings
steps.instructions: Access the monitor’s network configuration menu. Check that it has a valid IP address, subnet mask, and gateway for your hospital network. Confirm the EMR or central station IP and port settings match hospital network requirements.

steps.title: Ping / Connectivity Test
steps.instructions: Use a hospital PC or network tool to ping the monitor’s IP address. If the ping fails, confirm network switch configuration and VLAN assignment.

steps.title: Check for EMR / Central Station Issues
steps.instructions: Confirm the central station or EMR server is operational. Ensure no known network maintenance or outages are in progress. Verify other monitors can communicate to the same server.

steps.title: Restart the Monitor / Network Interface
steps.instructions: Safely power down the monitor, wait 10–15 seconds, and power it back on. Some network issues clear with a reboot.

steps.title: Swap Ports / Switches
steps.instructions: If the monitor still fails to communicate, try connecting it to a known working switch port. This helps isolate potential port or switch-level issues.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor data not appearing on central station; network error displayed.
CCR.Cause: Ethernet cable and switch port confirmed functional; monitor IP settings verified; monitor still fails to communicate.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent to Clinical Engineering bench for further evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Any error codes displayed on monitor

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Qube - Alarm Volume or Configuration Issues
description: Guide to troubleshoot Spacelabs Qube patient monitors with alarm volume or configuration problems, focusing on external settings before internal faults.

steps.title: Check Master Volume and Device Controls
steps.instructions: Verify the master volume setting on the monitor. Some models have physical knobs or menu-based volume settings. Confirm that the mute function is not activated. Why: Simple misadjustments are the most common cause of “silent” alarms.

steps.title: Inspect Alarm Configuration Settings
steps.instructions: Navigate to the alarm settings menu. Check high/low limits for vital signs. Ensure that alarm levels are properly configured for the intended patient population. Why: Incorrect limits may prevent audible alarms from triggering as expected.

steps.title: Confirm Patient-Specific Alarm Settings
steps.instructions: Verify that patient profiles do not have customized alarm suppression or reduced volume. Why: Individual patient configurations can override default alarm behavior.

steps.title: Check External Connections
steps.instructions: If external speakers or nurse call systems are connected, ensure cables are secure. Verify that the external audio device is powered on and functioning. Why: Loose or disconnected peripherals can cause alarms to fail externally.

steps.title: Test Alarm with Simulated Event
steps.instructions: Trigger a test alarm using the monitor’s built-in alarm test function. Listen for proper volume, tone, and duration. Why: Confirms whether the issue is with the monitor itself or user settings.

steps.title: Software / Firmware Verification
steps.instructions: Confirm the monitor is running the latest approved firmware version. Note any software-related alarms or error messages. Why: Firmware bugs can occasionally affect alarm behavior.

CCR.Complaint: User reported that the monitor’s alarms were inaudible or not triggering at set limits.
CCR.Cause: Alarm settings were found muted and patient-specific limits were set incorrectly. External speaker connection confirmed intact.
CCR.Resolution: Master and patient-specific alarm volumes were adjusted. Alarm limits verified. Alarm test successfully triggered audible alerts.

helpfulDetails: Any external speaker or nurse call connections
Any observed patterns (intermittent silence, only certain alarms affected)

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Qube - NIBP Measurement Errors or Failures
description: Guide for troubleshooting NIBP measurement errors or failures on a Spacelabs Qube patient monitor, emphasizing external checks before assuming internal failure.

steps.title: Check the Power and Display
steps.instructions: Confirm the monitor powers on and the display functions correctly. Why: A low battery or intermittent power can prevent NIBP cycles from completing.

steps.title: Verify Cuff Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect the NIBP hose connection to the monitor and cuff. Ensure it is fully seated and not loose. Check for kinks or sharp bends in the tubing. Why: Poor connections or blocked lines can prevent proper inflation/deflation.

steps.title: Inspect the Cuff
steps.instructions: Examine the cuff for tears, worn Velcro, or leaks. Confirm correct cuff size for the patient’s limb. Why: Damaged cuffs or improper sizing can trigger measurement failures or inaccurate readings.

steps.title: Check for Air Leaks
steps.instructions: Inflate the cuff manually (if possible) and listen for escaping air. Use a known good cuff on the same monitor to test functionality. Why: Air leaks reduce pressure buildup, causing failed readings.

steps.title: Monitor Placement and Patient Condition
steps.instructions: Ensure the cuff is applied to the correct limb and at heart level. Minimize patient movement during measurement. Why: Motion and improper positioning can interfere with NIBP oscillometric measurements.

steps.title: Run a Self-Test / NIBP Calibration (if available)
steps.instructions: Some Qube monitors allow an internal NIBP self-test via the menu. Why: Confirms whether the monitor can complete a cycle independently of patient factors.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor shows ‘Cuff Error’ and fails to measure blood pressure.
CCR.Cause: Cuff tubing found kinked; self-test failed with original cuff. Alternative cuff worked normally.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced damaged cuff. NIBP measurement successful with new cuff.

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Qube - ECG Lead Detection Errors / Waveform Issues
description: Guide to troubleshoot ECG lead detection errors and abnormal waveforms on a Spacelabs Qube patient monitor.

steps.title: Check patient connection
steps.instructions: Verify all ECG electrodes are attached correctly to the patient. Ensure skin is clean, dry, and free of oils or lotions. Replace any old or dried electrodes. Why: Poor electrode contact is the most common cause of lead detection errors.

steps.title: Inspect ECG lead cables
steps.instructions: Check for visible damage, frays, or broken connectors. Ensure cables are fully seated at both the patient side and monitor connector. Swap with a known good cable if available. Why: Damaged leads or loose connections can mimic waveform or lead errors.

steps.title: Confirm proper lead placement
steps.instructions: Verify standard lead positions (RA, LA, RL, LL, V1-V6 if applicable) are correct. Make sure limb leads are not crossed or touching each other. Why: Misplacement can cause waveform distortion or lead-off errors.

steps.title: Check monitor settings
steps.instructions: Verify ECG mode (3-lead, 5-lead, or 12-lead) matches the applied electrode configuration. Ensure filters are appropriately set (not excessively filtering small signals). Why: Incorrect configuration can cause the device to misinterpret signals.

steps.title: Observe for interference sources
steps.instructions: Ensure no large electrical devices or patient-worn pumps are near the monitor leads. Remove or reposition equipment if interference is suspected. Why: Electrical noise can cause erratic or missing waveforms.

steps.title: Power cycle the monitor
steps.instructions: Turn the device off and back on to reset lead detection circuits. Why: Software glitches may temporarily prevent proper lead detection.

CCR.Complaint: Nurse reports intermittent ECG waveform with “Lead Off” messages.
CCR.Cause: Observed dried electrodes and loose lead cable connections.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced electrodes and reseated leads; verified normal waveforms.

helpfulDetails: Check if multiple leads are affected or just one
Document which cables were tested or swapped
Record waveform quality before and after intervention
Note any unusual noise or patient movement affecting the signal

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Qube - Power-On / Boot Failure
description: Guide to troubleshoot a Spacelabs Qube patient monitor that fails to power on or complete its boot sequence, focusing on external checks before repair.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Never attempt troubleshooting on a monitor that is attached to a patient. Move the patient to a functioning monitor before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check the Power Source
steps.instructions: Verify that the wall outlet is supplying power (use a known working device or a multimeter). Confirm that the outlet is not switched off or on a tripped circuit. Why: Ensures the monitor isn’t failing due to missing mains power.

steps.title: Inspect the Power Cord and Connection
steps.instructions: Check for visible damage to the power cord or plug. Ensure the power cord is fully seated in both the monitor and the wall outlet. Why: Loose or damaged cords are common causes of no-power issues.

steps.title: Test with Battery (if equipped)
steps.instructions: If the Qube has a removable battery, check if the battery is seated correctly. Attempt to power on the device using battery only. Why: Confirms whether the issue is related to AC power or the battery.

steps.title: Reset External Power
steps.instructions: Disconnect the monitor from all power sources (battery and AC) for at least 30 seconds. Reconnect AC power (and battery if used) and attempt to power on. Why: Some monitors may lock in an unresponsive state and require a power reset.

steps.title: Check External Indicators
steps.instructions: Look for any LEDs that light briefly or flash during boot attempts. Note any audible error tones. Why: LED patterns or beeps can indicate specific hardware faults.

steps.title: Remove Non-Essential Accessories
steps.instructions: Disconnect external modules, sensors, or network cables. Attempt to boot the monitor with only essential components. Why: A failing accessory can prevent the monitor from completing boot.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor will not power on; display remains blank and no response to power button.
CCR.Cause: External power and battery verified; power reset and accessory removal did not restore function. Device fails to boot, indicating possible internal fault.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for bench repair.

helpfulDetails: Power cord inspected and confirmed intact
LED indicators or error beeps noted
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Sentinel Central Station - Bedside Monitor Communication Dropout
description: Loss of communication between bedside monitors and central station, including intermittent dropouts, missing waveforms, or alarms not appearing centrally.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is being actively monitored at the bedside. Do not rely on central station visibility during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Bedside Monitor is Powered and Active
steps.instructions: Check that the bedside monitor is ON, not in standby, and actively displaying patient data. If the monitor is off or frozen, resolve locally first.

steps.title: Check Network Connectivity Indicator on Bedside Monitor
steps.instructions: Look for network/communication icons or messages (e.g., disconnected, searching, or no network). A visible disconnect indicates a network issue rather than central station failure.

steps.title: Confirm Central Station is Operational
steps.instructions: Ensure the Sentinel central station is powered on, application is running, and not frozen. Restart application if unresponsive.

steps.title: Check for Multiple Monitor Dropouts
steps.instructions: Determine if the issue affects: One monitor → likely local issue; Multiple monitors → likely network or server issue. This helps narrow scope quickly.

steps.title: Inspect Network Cables (Wired Systems)
steps.instructions: Ensure Ethernet cable is securely connected at bedside monitor and wall port. Look for damaged or loose cables. Reseat connections. Loose or damaged cables are a common cause of dropouts.

steps.title: Verify Network Switch Port Activity
steps.instructions: Check link/activity lights at the network port or switch (if accessible). No activity suggests a port, cable, or network issue.

steps.title: Check Wireless Signal (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: For wireless monitors: confirm signal strength indicator and check for known dead zones. Reposition monitor if needed. Weak signal commonly causes intermittent dropouts.

steps.title: Confirm Correct Network Assignment / Unit Configuration
steps.instructions: Verify the bedside monitor is assigned to the correct unit/central station. Misconfiguration can cause the monitor to not appear centrally.

steps.title: Restart Bedside Monitor (If Safe)
steps.instructions: Power cycle the bedside monitor after ensuring patient is safely monitored elsewhere. This can resolve temporary communication or software issues.

steps.title: Restart Central Station Application or Workstation
steps.instructions: If multiple monitors are affected, restart the central station software or workstation (if approved). Clears software/network session issues.

steps.title: Check Hospital Network Status
steps.instructions: Confirm with IT if there are network outages, VLAN issues, or server/interface problems. Central monitoring relies heavily on hospital network infrastructure.

steps.title: Verify Server / Interface Connectivity
steps.instructions: If applicable, confirm communication with backend servers (e.g., Spacelabs network server). Loss here will affect multiple devices.

steps.title: Observe for Recurring Dropouts
steps.instructions: Monitor for patterns (specific room, time, or movement-related). Patterns often point to infrastructure issues.

CCR.Complaint: Central station not displaying patient data from bedside monitor in Room 12.
CCR.Cause: Loose Ethernet cable at wall port causing intermittent network disconnect.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated and secured network cable; confirmed stable communication to central station.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (working, removed from service, escalated)

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Sentinel Central Station - Alarm Not Propagating from Bedside to Central
description: Identifies causes when bedside alarms do not appear on the central station, including connectivity, configuration, or external interference issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm patients are being actively monitored at the bedside. If alarms are silenced or inhibited, move patient care to a safe monitor before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Bedside Monitor Alarm Settings
steps.instructions:
Action: Check that the bedside monitor’s alarms are enabled and volume is audible.
Expected Outcome: Alarms should be active; disabled alarms will not propagate.

steps.title: Inspect Network Connectivity
steps.instructions:
Action: Confirm bedside monitor is connected to the correct network and shows a valid IP.
Expected Outcome: No network errors; a disconnected monitor cannot send alarms to central.

steps.title: Check Physical Connections
steps.instructions:
Action: Inspect network cables and patch panels; ensure connections are secure and undamaged.
Expected Outcome: Solid connection; loose or damaged cables can interrupt alarm signals.

steps.title: Review Central Station Configuration
steps.instructions:
Action: Verify the central station has the correct bedside monitor assigned and is configured to receive alarms.
Expected Outcome: Correct assignments; misconfigured central stations will not display alarms.

steps.title: Examine Software or Server Status
steps.instructions:
Action: Check central station and associated servers for software errors, status indicators, or messages.
Expected Outcome: No errors; software issues may prevent alarm propagation.

steps.title: Swap Bedside Monitor (if possible)
steps.instructions:
Action: Replace with a known working monitor.
Expected Outcome: If alarms propagate with a different monitor, bedside unit may require repair.

steps.title: Check for Intermittent Network Issues
steps.instructions:
Action: Ping bedside monitor from central station or network tool; observe packet loss or high latency.
Expected Outcome: Stable network; intermittent network issues can block alarm signals.

CCR.Complaint: Bedside monitor alarms not appearing on central station.
CCR.Cause: Bedside monitor correctly connected, alarms enabled, but central station not receiving signals; network stable.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for bench evaluation.

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Sentinel Central Station - Display or Workstation Hardware Failure
description: Identifies causes when the central station display or workstation fails, freezes, or shows visual artifacts, including hardware, power, or peripheral issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm patients are being actively monitored at the bedside. Move monitoring to another station if necessary.

steps.title: Check Power and Cables
steps.instructions: Verify AC power is connected and outlet is live. Inspect power cords for damage or loose connections. A display may fail if power is unstable.

steps.title: Inspect Workstation Indicators
steps.instructions: Check for LEDs, fan operation, and audible alerts. No lights or fan movement may indicate a power or motherboard issue.

steps.title: Confirm Peripheral Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure keyboard, mouse, and monitor cables are fully seated. Loose connections can cause workstation unresponsiveness or display failure.

steps.title: Reboot the Workstation Safely
steps.instructions: If safe, perform a controlled reboot. Observe if the display initializes correctly. Successful reboot may resolve temporary software or firmware hang-ups.

steps.title: Swap Monitor or Workstation Components
steps.instructions: If available, connect a known-good monitor or workstation to verify if the failure follows the hardware or remains with the station.

steps.title: Check for Network or Server Impact
steps.instructions: Confirm the central station is communicating with network servers. Some failures may appear as display issues but are caused by server or network communication problems.

CCR.Complaint: Central station display frozen; no response from keyboard or mouse.
CCR.Cause: Power and peripherals verified; workstation unresponsive after reboot.
CCR.Resolution: Station removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair.

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Sentinel Central Station - Network or server communication errors
description: Identifies causes for lost or delayed data from bedside monitors, missing alarms, or connection errors due to network, server, or configuration issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm all patients are actively monitored at the bedside. If needed, move monitoring to backup devices to prevent interruptions.

steps.title: Check Network Connectivity
steps.instructions:
Verify the central station is connected to the hospital network.
Inspect Ethernet cables and patch panels for secure connections.
Expected outcome: network link lights are active; no intermittent drops.

steps.title: Ping Central Station from Server/Workstation
steps.instructions:
Use network tools to ping the central station and confirm IP connectivity.
Expected outcome: consistent response; no timeouts.

steps.title: Check Server Status and Services
steps.instructions:
Confirm Spacelabs server or database is operational.
Restart relevant services if safe and authorized.
Expected outcome: services report as running; central station reconnects.

steps.title: Verify Configuration Settings
steps.instructions:
Confirm IP, subnet mask, gateway, and port settings match hospital network requirements.
Ensure firewall or VLAN settings allow communication.
Expected outcome: no configuration mismatch; device can reach server.

steps.title: Examine Logs for Errors
steps.instructions:
Review central station and server logs for recent network errors, failed authentication, or dropped packets.
Expected outcome: errors indicate whether the issue is network or server-side.

steps.title: Test with Alternate Network Port or Switch
steps.instructions:
Move central station to a known-good network port or switch to rule out hardware network issues.
Expected outcome: stable communication if original port or switch was faulty.

CCR.Complaint: Central station intermittently losing connection to server; alarms not received.
CCR.Cause: Network cables and switch port tested; configuration settings verified; issue persists.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service; labeled Out of Service; sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Final device status labeled Out of Service

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Sentinel Central Station - Database or Storage Failures
description: Identifies causes for database corruption, missing historical data, or storage errors impacting central station performance or patient monitoring records.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm all patients are actively monitored at the bedside. If necessary, move monitoring to alternative devices before troubleshooting storage issues to avoid data gaps.

steps.title: Check System Status and Alerts
steps.instructions: Review the Sentinel’s status screens for any database or storage warnings. Look for red or amber alerts, disk space warnings, or RAID failures.

steps.title: Verify Power and Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure the central station has stable power and is not experiencing brownouts. Confirm all storage devices (internal or external) are securely connected. Unstable power can corrupt databases.

steps.title: Check Available Storage Space
steps.instructions: Confirm sufficient free disk space. A full database drive can prevent data writes and cause errors. Expected outcome: free space above the manufacturer’s recommended minimum.

steps.title: Review Recent System Changes
steps.instructions: Check for recent software updates, configuration changes, or unexpected shutdowns. These can cause database inconsistencies or partial writes.

steps.title: Verify Network Storage (if applicable)
steps.instructions: If the database resides on a network server, ensure network connectivity is stable and permissions allow read/write access. Look for latency or dropped packets that can affect storage operations.

steps.title: Attempt a Soft Database Recovery (if supported)
steps.instructions: Use Sentinel’s software tools to verify or repair the database. Expected outcome: database consistency check passes, or partial repair succeeds. Do not perform full reinstallation unless directed by escalation.

steps.title: Check for Unusual Sounds, Heat, or Smells
steps.instructions: Listen for abnormal disk activity (clicking, grinding) and check for excessive heat. These are signs of impending storage hardware failure and require immediate escalation.

CCR.Complaint: Central station displaying database errors; historical patient data inaccessible.
CCR.Cause: Disk nearly full with corrupted database files; RAID warning present.
CCR.Resolution: Verified storage connections, freed disk space, attempted software database repair; station flagged Out of Service and sent for vendor evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Station operational status after attempted repair

title: Spacelabs Healthcare 90207 Patient Monitor - Cuff Not Inflating or Low Pressure Errors
description: Troubleshooting cuff inflation and low pressure errors caused by leaks, tubing, cuff issues, or pump faults.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the cuff from the patient before troubleshooting to prevent discomfort and inaccurate readings.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Start a manual BP measurement and observe if the pump inflates, pressure builds, or errors appear.

steps.title: Inspect the Cuff for Leaks or Damage
steps.instructions: Check for tears, punctures, or worn Velcro and swap with a known good cuff if needed.

steps.title: Check the Air Hose and Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure tubing is secure, not cracked or kinked, and swap if necessary.

steps.title: Verify Proper Cuff Size and Application
steps.instructions: Ensure correct cuff size, snug fit, and proper artery alignment.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions in the Air Path
steps.instructions: Confirm tubing is not pinched and connectors are free of debris.

steps.title: Listen for Pump Operation
steps.instructions: Listen for pump activity during inflation to determine if the pump is functioning.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Restart the device and retest to clear minor firmware or control issues.

CCR.Complaint: Unit reported with cuff not inflating and low pressure error during BP cycle.
CCR.Cause: Air leak identified in cuff tubing causing inability to maintain pressure.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced cuff and tubing assembly; verified proper inflation and normal BP readings.

title: Spacelabs Healthcare 90207 Patient Monitor - Battery Not Charging
description: Device not charging, not powering on, or shutting down due to battery, charger, or connection issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from the patient before troubleshooting. Monitoring is stopped safely or transferred to another device to prevent interruption of patient data collection and ensure safe handling.

steps.title: Verify Charging Method and Equipment
steps.instructions: Confirm the correct charging dock or AC adapter is being used. Ensure the correct Spacelabs charger is used and confirm compatibility with no third-party chargers.

steps.title: Check Power Source (Outlet)
steps.instructions: Verify the wall outlet is functional by testing with another device or trying a different known working outlet.

steps.title: Inspect Charger and Power Cord
steps.instructions: Check for frayed cables, bent prongs, and ensure connections are fully seated. Swap with a known good charger if available.

steps.title: Inspect Device Charging Contacts
steps.instructions: Inspect for dirt, corrosion, or bent contacts and clean gently if needed to ensure proper electrical contact.

steps.title: Confirm Charging Indicators
steps.instructions: Place the device on the charger and observe for charging LED or screen indicators, noting any lack of response or intermittent behavior.

steps.title: Allow Charging Time and Recheck
steps.instructions: Leave the device charging for at least 30–60 minutes and attempt to power on afterward to confirm battery response.

steps.title: Perform Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Remove from charger, power off if possible, then power back on and reconnect to charger to clear potential firmware issues.

steps.title: Swap with Known Good Battery (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Install a known good battery if supported and reattempt charging to determine if the issue is battery-related.

CCR.Complaint: Device not charging and would not power on during setup for patient use.
CCR.Cause: Failed internal battery after confirming good charger, outlet, and connections.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent for battery replacement and evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Battery behavior (no charge vs short runtime)

title: Spacelabs Healthcare 90207 Patient Monitor - Display or Keypad Failure
description: Troubleshooting unresponsive screen or keypad issues caused by power, battery, or external interface problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the monitor is in use, remove it from the patient and replace it with a functioning unit before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Power Status
steps.instructions: Press the power button and observe for any screen activity, beeps, or indicator lights.

steps.title: Check Battery Condition and Installation
steps.instructions: Ensure batteries are installed correctly, replace with known good batteries, and check for corrosion or loose contacts.

steps.title: Inspect External Power Adapter (If Used)
steps.instructions: Ensure adapter is connected, test outlet, and swap with a known good adapter if available.

steps.title: Assess Display Behavior
steps.instructions: Check for dim, flickering, or partial screen function to determine display condition.

steps.title: Test Keypad Responsiveness
steps.instructions: Press multiple buttons and observe for beeps or screen response.

steps.title: Perform a Power Reset
steps.instructions: Remove batteries, wait 30–60 seconds, reinstall, and power on.

steps.title: Check for Environmental or Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect for cracked screen, liquid exposure, or damaged buttons.

CCR.Complaint: Spacelabs 90207 display blank and keypad not responding during setup.
CCR.Cause: Failed to power on due to depleted batteries.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced batteries and verified normal display and keypad function.

title: Spacelabs Healthcare 90207 Patient Monitor - Time Clock or Scheduling Errors
description: Troubleshooting incorrect time, missed readings, and scheduling issues on the Spacelabs 90207 caused by configuration or power-related problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the monitor is actively being used for a study, assess whether incorrect timing affects clinical accuracy. Transition to another unit if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Check current displayed date/time, compare with a known correct time, and review programmed BP schedule intervals.

steps.title: Check Time and Date Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm correct date, time, AM/PM settings, and time zone if applicable. Adjust manually if incorrect.

steps.title: Verify BP Scheduling Configuration
steps.instructions: Check daytime and nighttime intervals, confirm monitoring start and stop times, and ensure schedule matches intended study duration.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the device off, remove and reinsert batteries, then power back on and verify time.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Replace with fresh batteries and observe whether time resets after battery removal.

steps.title: Check for Time Reset After Power Loss
steps.instructions: Remove batteries briefly, reinstall, and check if time reverts to default.

steps.title: Test Scheduling Function
steps.instructions: Program a short interval schedule and verify readings occur at the correct times.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported incorrect timestamps and missed BP readings during ambulatory study.
CCR.Cause: Device clock reset after battery removal, indicating internal timekeeping failure.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced batteries and tested; issue persisted. Unit removed from service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Time retention after power loss checked
Final device status (in service vs out of service)

title: Spacelabs Healthcare 90207 Patient Monitor - Memory Full or Data Download Failure
description: Memory full errors, inability to download study data, or failed communication between monitor and computer due to connection, software, or workflow issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the monitor is still assigned to a patient, confirm the study is complete and the device is no longer in use before proceeding. Expected: Device is safe to handle without interrupting patient care. Why it matters: Prevents loss of active study data or interruption of monitoring.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Power on the unit and check for messages such as “Memory Full” or failed download attempts. Attempt to initiate a data download and observe any error messages. Expected: Issue is reproducible and clearly identified. Why it matters: Confirms whether the problem is storage-related or communication-related.

steps.title: Check Device Memory Status
steps.instructions: Navigate the device menu to confirm stored studies, verify number of recordings, and identify if memory is at capacity. Expected: Memory shows full or near full capacity. Why it matters: The 90207 has limited onboard storage and must be cleared between uses.

steps.title: Confirm Proper Docking/Interface Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect the connection between the monitor and the interface. Ensure proper seating, secure cables, and no damage. Expected: Solid physical connection with no damage. Why it matters: Poor connection prevents successful data transfer.

steps.title: Verify Computer Software and Communication
steps.instructions: Check host system software, confirm it is functioning, verify correct port selection, and restart if needed. Expected: Software recognizes the device when connected. Why it matters: Communication failures are often due to incorrect configuration.

steps.title: Attempt Data Download Again
steps.instructions: Initiate the download with confirmed setup and monitor for successful transfer. Expected: Data transfers successfully. Why it matters: Confirms issue resolution or persistence.

steps.title: Clear Device Memory After Successful Download
steps.instructions: Confirm study is saved and clear device memory. Expected: Memory is cleared and ready for reuse. Why it matters: Prevents recurring errors.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn off device, reseat batteries, restart, and retry download. Expected: Communication may restore. Why it matters: Clears temporary faults.

steps.title: Test with Known Good Accessories
steps.instructions: Try alternate docking station, cable, or workstation. Expected: Successful communication on alternate setup. Why it matters: Isolates accessory vs device issue.

CCR.Complaint: Unit would not download data and displayed memory full error after patient use.
CCR.Cause: Device memory full and failed communication due to faulty docking interface connection.
CCR.Resolution: Re-seated device, completed data download, cleared memory, and verified proper operation.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (in service or sent for repair)

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Xprezzon Patient Monitor - No display / monitor will not power on
description: Troubleshooting a Spacelabs Xprezzon patient monitor with no display, no power, or failed startup from external causes.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the monitor is connected to a patient and the display is blank or the monitor will not power on, do not continue relying on it for patient monitoring. Move the patient to a known-good monitor or bedside monitoring setup according to clinical procedure. Expected outcome: Patient monitoring is restored using a reliable device before troubleshooting begins. Why it matters: A patient monitor with no display cannot be trusted to provide alarms, waveforms, numerics, or parameter status.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Power Failure
steps.instructions: Press the power button and observe the monitor closely. Check for: Any screen backlight; Spacelabs startup screen; Power indicator light; Battery or charging indicator; Audible tones; Fan noise, relay click, or internal startup sound. Expected outcome: You determine whether the monitor is completely dead or powering internally with no usable display. If the monitor starts normally, verify operation and return it to service if no fault repeats.

steps.title: Check the Wall Outlet
steps.instructions: Confirm the AC outlet is live using an outlet tester or another known-good device. Avoid assuming the monitor is faulty until the power source is verified. Expected outcome: The outlet provides proper power. If the outlet is dead or controlled by a wall switch, move the monitor to a verified outlet and retest. If the monitor powers on, stop troubleshooting and document the outlet issue.

steps.title: Inspect the AC Power Cord
steps.instructions: Check that the power cord is fully seated at both the wall outlet and the monitor or external power supply. Inspect the cord for: Cuts; Loose plug blades; Crushed insulation; Bent connector; Evidence of liquid exposure; Missing hospital-grade plug integrity. Expected outcome: The power cord is secure and physically safe to use. If the cord is damaged, remove it from use, replace it with an approved known-good cord, and retest.

steps.title: Swap with a Known-Good Power Cord
steps.instructions: Use a verified compatible hospital-grade power cord. Make sure the cord fits securely and does not wiggle at the monitor connection. Expected outcome: The monitor powers on with the known-good cord. If the monitor powers on, the original cord was the likely cause. Replace the cord, verify startup, and stop.

steps.title: Check for an External Power Supply or Power Module Issue
steps.instructions: If the Xprezzon configuration uses an external AC adapter or power module, inspect it for: Loose input or output cable; No power indicator light; Cracked housing; Burnt smell; Unusual heat; Damaged strain relief. Expected outcome: The power supply appears intact and is providing power. If available, test with a known-good compatible power supply approved for that monitor configuration. If the monitor powers on with the known-good supply, remove the failed supply from service and replace it.

steps.title: Check Battery Status
steps.instructions: If the monitor has a removable or internal battery-supported configuration, connect it to verified AC power and allow it to sit briefly before attempting startup again. Observe for a charging indicator or battery status message. Expected outcome: The monitor recognizes AC power and begins charging or starts normally. If the monitor only fails on battery but powers on with AC, document reduced battery operation and send for battery evaluation or replacement according to facility procedure.

steps.title: Remove Nonessential Accessories
steps.instructions: Disconnect external accessories that are not required for basic startup, such as: External display cables; USB devices; Recorder modules; Network cable; Parameter cables; External keyboard or barcode accessories, if present. Then reconnect only AC power and attempt startup. Expected outcome: The monitor powers on without accessory interference. If the monitor powers on after an accessory is removed, reconnect accessories one at a time to identify the cause. Remove the suspect accessory from service.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage or Fluid Intrusion
steps.instructions: Look over the monitor housing, screen, connectors, handle, and rear panel. Check for: Cracked case; Damaged power inlet; Sticky residue; Signs of cleaning fluid intrusion; Corrosion at connectors; Burnt odor; Unusual heat. Expected outcome: No visible safety concern is present. If fluid intrusion, smoke odor, overheating, or power inlet damage is found, do not continue power testing. Remove the monitor from service.

steps.title: Check Display Behavior Separately from Power Behavior
steps.instructions: If the monitor appears to power internally but the screen remains blank, look for signs that the device is running: Alarm sounds; Button response; Indicator lights; Network activity; External display output, if configured; Parameter module activity. Expected outcome: You determine whether the issue is no power or possible display/backlight failure. If the monitor is running but the display remains blank, remove it from service for bench evaluation. Do not return a patient monitor with an unreliable display.

steps.title: Perform a Safe Power Reset
steps.instructions: Disconnect AC power. If the battery is externally removable and facility policy allows removal, remove it. Wait briefly, reconnect AC power, reinstall the battery if removed, and attempt startup again. Expected outcome: The monitor restarts normally after a basic power reset. If the monitor powers on and completes startup normally, verify stable operation, charging status, and display function before returning it to service.

steps.title: Verify Full Startup Before Returning to Service
steps.instructions: If the monitor powers on, confirm: Display is bright and stable; Startup completes without error; AC power indicator works; Battery indicator behaves normally; Basic controls respond; Alarm audio is present; No burning smell, overheating, or intermittent shutdown occurs. Expected outcome: The monitor operates normally and safely. If the issue cannot be reproduced after correcting an external cause, document the checks performed and return to service only if the monitor passes functional verification.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Spacelabs Xprezzon patient monitor had a blank screen and would not power on at the bedside.
CCR.Cause: Verified wall outlet was functional, but the monitor remained off with the original power cord and powered on normally with a known-good hospital-grade cord.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced the defective power cord, confirmed normal startup, stable display, AC power indication, and returned the monitor to service.

helpfulDetails: AC adapter or power supply indicator status
Whether the screen was completely blank or dimly lit
Any startup tones, fan noise, or indicator lights
Signs of fluid intrusion, heat, smell, or physical damage
Whether issue occurred on AC power, battery power, or both

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Xprezzon Patient Monitor - ECG Lead Fail, Artifact, or Noisy Waveform
description: Troubleshooting ECG lead fail, artifact, unstable waveform, or noisy ECG display caused by setup, cable, electrode, or connection issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the monitor is connected to an active patient and ECG monitoring is unreliable, notify clinical staff immediately. Do not rely on a noisy or intermittent ECG waveform for rhythm assessment until the signal is corrected. If continuous monitoring is required, move the patient to another verified monitor or alternate monitoring source before troubleshooting. Expected outcome: Patient monitoring is maintained safely before equipment troubleshooting begins.

steps.title: Verify the Reported ECG Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm the displayed problem on the Xprezzon monitor. Look for: ECG lead fail message; flat ECG trace; excessive artifact; wandering baseline; intermittent waveform dropout; noisy waveform only on one lead; noisy waveform on all displayed ECG leads. Expected outcome: The reported ECG issue is confirmed and the behavior is clearly identified. If the issue is not present, document that the monitor passed functional observation and ask staff when the issue occurs.

steps.title: Check the Patient and Electrode Setup
steps.instructions: Inspect electrode placement and skin contact. Verify that electrodes are: fresh; properly adhered; not dried out; not loose at the snap connection; applied to clean, dry skin; not placed over excessive hair, lotion, sweat, or broken skin. Expected outcome: Poor electrode contact is corrected and ECG waveform stabilizes. If replacing or reseating electrodes resolves the issue, return the monitor to service and stop.

steps.title: Check for Motion or Muscle Artifact
steps.instructions: Observe whether the artifact appears during patient movement, shivering, tremors, coughing, bed movement, or patient handling. Ask staff whether the waveform becomes clean when the patient is still. Expected outcome: Motion-related artifact is identified as a clinical or setup condition rather than a monitor failure. If the ECG waveform is stable when the patient is still, document motion artifact and return the monitor to service if all hardware checks pass.

steps.title: Inspect the ECG Lead Wires
steps.instructions: Visually inspect each ECG lead wire from the patient end to the trunk cable connection. Check for: cracked insulation; bent pins; loose snaps; stretched wires; fluid contamination; broken strain relief; intermittent signal when the wire is gently moved. Expected outcome: Damaged or intermittent lead wires are identified. Replace the ECG lead set if damage or intermittency is found. If the waveform clears with a known-good lead set, return the monitor to service and stop.

steps.title: Check the ECG Trunk Cable
steps.instructions: Inspect the ECG trunk cable connection at the monitor or input module. Confirm the cable is fully seated and not partially connected. Gently flex the cable near the connector and strain relief while observing the ECG waveform. Expected outcome: The ECG signal remains stable while the cable is handled. If the waveform cuts in and out, alarms lead fail, or becomes noisy when the trunk cable is moved, replace the trunk cable or remove the device from service for further evaluation.

steps.title: Swap With a Known-Good ECG Cable Set
steps.instructions: Replace the ECG lead wires and trunk cable with a known-good compatible ECG cable set. Use fresh electrodes during the test to avoid carrying over a setup problem. Expected outcome: A known-good cable set produces a clean ECG waveform without lead fail. If the issue resolves, the original ECG cable or lead set was the likely cause. Replace the defective accessory and return the monitor to service.

steps.title: Check Whether the Issue Follows the Cable or Stays With the Monitor
steps.instructions: If possible, test the suspected ECG cable set on another compatible monitor or test the Xprezzon monitor with a known-good cable set. Expected outcome: If the problem follows the cable, replace the cable or lead wires. If the problem stays with the monitor, suspect the monitor ECG input path, parameter module, or internal hardware. This helps avoid sending a good monitor for repair when the accessory is the true cause.

steps.title: Check Monitor ECG Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm the ECG source, lead selection, size, filter, and monitoring mode are appropriate for patient monitoring. Make sure the staff is not viewing a lead with poor electrode contact while other leads are acceptable. Expected outcome: ECG display settings are not causing the waveform to appear poor or misleading. If adjusting the displayed lead or ECG size resolves the concern, document the settings correction and return the monitor to service.

steps.title: Check for Nearby Electrical Interference
steps.instructions: Look for possible interference sources near the monitor or patient cables. Examples include: damaged power cords; electric beds; warming devices; sequential compression devices; poorly routed ECG cables near power supplies; loose grounding or questionable outlet conditions. Route ECG cables away from power cords and high-current accessories when possible. Expected outcome: External interference is reduced and the ECG waveform becomes cleaner.

steps.title: Inspect the Monitor Input Area
steps.instructions: Check the ECG input connector area for obvious external damage, bent pins, contamination, looseness, or evidence of fluid intrusion. Do not open the monitor or perform internal board-level troubleshooting during floor support. Expected outcome: No visible external damage or contamination is present. If the ECG input appears damaged, loose, contaminated, or fluid-exposed, remove the monitor from service.

steps.title: Perform a Bench Check With an ECG Simulator
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from patient use and test the ECG function with a known-good ECG simulator and known-good ECG cable set. Confirm that the Xprezzon displays a stable simulated ECG waveform without lead fail or excessive artifact. Expected outcome: The monitor displays a clean simulated ECG signal. If the monitor passes simulator testing, the original issue was likely related to electrodes, patient movement, cable condition, or environmental interference. If the monitor fails simulator testing with known-good accessories, remove it from service for repair evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported ECG lead fail and noisy ECG waveform on a Spacelabs Xprezzon patient monitor during patient monitoring.
CCR.Cause: Found loose ECG electrode contact and intermittent artifact when the ECG lead wire set was flexed near the patient lead connection.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced ECG electrodes and swapped the ECG lead wire set with a known-good set; monitor displayed stable ECG waveform and passed ECG simulator check.

helpfulDetails: Patient was moved to another monitor before troubleshooting
Skin prep or electrode contact issue found
Artifact occurred with movement or cable flexing

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Xprezzon Patient Monitor - NIBP Will Not Inflate or Gives Measurement Error
description: Troubleshooting NIBP inflation failure, cuff connection problems, incorrect cuff sizing, hose leaks, and repeated non-invasive blood pressure measurement errors.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the monitor is being used on an active patient and NIBP readings are needed for care decisions, notify clinical staff before troubleshooting. Do not repeatedly cycle the cuff on the patient if the monitor is failing, overinflating, or producing repeated errors. Use another verified monitor or manual blood pressure method if accurate blood pressure monitoring is needed immediately. Expected outcome: Patient care is supported without relying on a questionable NIBP function.

steps.title: Verify the Reported NIBP Problem
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact complaint with staff. Check whether the issue is: Cuff does not inflate at all; Cuff inflates then immediately deflates; Measurement times out; Measurement error appears; Reading is inconsistent with clinical assessment; Error occurs only with one cuff, hose, or patient room. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified before parts are swapped.

steps.title: Check the Patient and Cuff Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm the cuff is properly wrapped, positioned, and sized for the patient’s limb. Check that the cuff index line falls within the acceptable range markings. Inspect for loose wrapping, clothing under the cuff, bent tubing, or patient movement during the measurement. Expected outcome: The cuff is correctly applied and the monitor has a reasonable chance of completing a measurement. If proper cuff placement resolves the issue, return the monitor to service and document the correction.

steps.title: Inspect the NIBP Cuff
steps.instructions: Remove the cuff and inspect it for: Torn bladder; Worn Velcro; Cracked connector; Kinked tubing; Stretched or leaking cuff material; Fluid contamination. Swap to a known-good compatible cuff. Expected outcome: If the cuff was defective, the monitor inflates normally and completes a reading with the replacement cuff. If the issue is resolved, replace the defective cuff and stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect the NIBP Hose
steps.instructions: Check the hose from the monitor to the cuff for visible damage. Look for pinches, cracks, loose fittings, stretched sections, or damaged O-rings at the connector. Reconnect both ends firmly. Swap with a known-good compatible NIBP hose if available. Expected outcome: A damaged or leaking hose is ruled out before suspecting the monitor. If the replacement hose restores normal operation, remove the bad hose from use.

steps.title: Check the Monitor’s NIBP Connector Port
steps.instructions: Inspect the NIBP port on the Xprezzon monitor. Look for: Bent or damaged fitting; Loose connector; Broken latch or locking point; Debris inside the port; Evidence of fluid intrusion. Do not force the connector into place. Expected outcome: The hose seats securely and the NIBP connection is not visibly damaged.

steps.title: Run a Test Measurement Using Known-Good Accessories
steps.instructions: Connect a known-good cuff and hose to the monitor. Perform a test NIBP cycle on an approved test setup, simulator, or appropriate non-patient test method per facility policy. Expected outcome: The cuff inflates, holds pressure long enough to measure, deflates normally, and displays a completed reading. If the monitor works with known-good accessories, the original cuff, hose, or setup was the likely cause.

steps.title: Check for Air Leak Behavior
steps.instructions: Start an NIBP cycle and observe the inflation pattern. If the pump runs but pressure does not build, suspect a cuff, hose, connector, or internal pneumatic leak. If pressure builds briefly and then drops quickly, check again for loose fittings or leaking accessories. Expected outcome: External pneumatic leaks are identified before the device is removed for internal evaluation.

steps.title: Check for Pump or Valve Behavior
steps.instructions: Listen during the NIBP cycle. Note whether: No pump sound is heard; Pump sounds weak; Pump runs continuously without pressure buildup; Cuff inflates but does not deflate normally; Cuff deflates immediately after starting. Expected outcome: The work order includes useful symptom detail for repair evaluation. Do not open the monitor or attempt internal pump, valve, or pressure transducer repair during floor troubleshooting.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Monitor
steps.instructions: If the issue appears intermittent or software-related, remove the monitor from active patient use first. Power the monitor off, allow it to fully shut down, then restart it. Reconnect the known-good NIBP hose and cuff and repeat the test. Expected outcome: A temporary control issue may clear, but repeated NIBP errors should not be ignored.

steps.title: Review Configuration or Patient Mode
steps.instructions: Confirm the monitor is in the correct patient category or care area configuration if available. Incorrect patient mode, cuff size, or clinical setup can contribute to measurement failure or inappropriate inflation limits. Expected outcome: The monitor setup matches the intended patient population and test condition.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Monitor Can Return to Service
steps.instructions: The monitor may return to service only if it repeatedly completes NIBP measurements with known-good accessories and no abnormal inflation, deflation, or error behavior is observed. If the NIBP function remains unreliable, remove the monitor from service.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the Spacelabs Xprezzon monitor would not inflate the NIBP cuff and displayed repeated measurement errors.
CCR.Cause: Troubleshooting found the original NIBP hose had a loose connector and would not maintain pressure during inflation.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced the NIBP hose with a known-good compatible hose, verified successful cuff inflation and repeated NIBP measurements, and returned the monitor to service.

helpfulDetails: Whether the issue occurred on a patient or during setup
Exact NIBP error or message displayed
Whether the cuff inflated, partially inflated, or did not inflate
Whether cuff and hose were swapped
Whether pressure appeared to leak down
Any abnormal noise from the pump
Any visible damage to the NIBP port

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Xprezzon Patient Monitor - Module Not Recognized or Parameter Missing
description: Troubleshooting missing parameters or unrecognized modules caused by seating, cabling, configuration, accessory, or module communication issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the Xprezzon monitor is connected to an active patient and a required parameter is missing, do not rely on incomplete monitoring. Move the patient to another verified monitor or provide alternate monitoring according to clinical policy before troubleshooting. Expected outcome: The patient is monitored safely before any device checks begin.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Missing Parameter
steps.instructions: Confirm what clinical staff reported as missing, such as ECG, SpO2, NIBP, invasive pressure, temperature, CO2, or another parameter. Check whether the parameter is missing from the screen, showing dashes, unavailable, or not appearing after module connection. Expected outcome: The specific missing parameter and symptom are clearly identified.

steps.title: Check Module Seating
steps.instructions: Inspect the affected module and confirm it is fully inserted into the monitor or module housing. Remove and reinsert the module firmly without forcing it. Watch for module indicator lights, screen changes, or parameter tiles returning. Expected outcome: If the issue was poor seating, the monitor recognizes the module and the parameter returns. If resolved, stop here.

steps.title: Inspect the Module Bay or Connector Area
steps.instructions: Check the module connection area for bent pins, debris, fluid residue, damaged plastic, or signs of impact. Do not use sharp tools or force the connector. Expected outcome: The module bay appears clean, dry, and physically intact.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Monitor Safely
steps.instructions: If the monitor is not connected to an active patient, power the Xprezzon monitor off, wait briefly, and power it back on. Observe whether the monitor detects the module during startup. Expected outcome: A temporary communication issue may clear after restart. If the module is recognized, verify normal operation and stop.

steps.title: Try a Known-Good Module of the Same Type
steps.instructions: Swap the suspect module with a known-good compatible Spacelabs module, if available. Use the same monitor and module slot. Expected outcome: If the known-good module works, the original module may be faulty. If the known-good module also fails, the issue may involve the monitor, module bay, configuration, or communication path.

steps.title: Try the Suspect Module on Another Compatible Monitor
steps.instructions: Install the suspect module on another known-good compatible Spacelabs monitor or module housing. Expected outcome: If the module works elsewhere, the original monitor or module bay should be evaluated. If the module fails on another monitor, the module should be removed from service.

steps.title: Check Patient Cables and Sensors
steps.instructions: Confirm the correct patient cable, lead set, SpO2 sensor, temperature probe, pressure cable, or CO2 accessory is connected. Inspect for damaged cable jackets, broken pins, loose connectors, fluid contamination, or incorrect accessory type. Replace with known-good accessories when available. Expected outcome: If the parameter was missing due to an accessory fault, the parameter should return after cable or sensor replacement.

steps.title: Confirm the Parameter Is Enabled or Displayed
steps.instructions: Review the monitor layout and parameter display settings to confirm the parameter is not hidden, minimized, or assigned to another screen area. Avoid making clinical configuration changes unless authorized by facility policy. Expected outcome: The parameter appears if it was hidden or not selected for display.

steps.title: Check for Alarm or Message Indicators
steps.instructions: Look for monitor messages such as module error, parameter unavailable, cable off, sensor off, lead fail, or communication error. Document the exact wording. Expected outcome: Any displayed message helps narrow whether the issue is module detection, accessory connection, or parameter configuration.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical or Environmental Damage
steps.instructions: Check the monitor and module for signs of drops, cracked housing, fluid ingress, corrosion, unusual heat, burning smell, or intermittent operation when gently handled. Expected outcome: Any evidence of damage supports removal from service and repair evaluation.

steps.title: Confirm Final Status
steps.instructions: After reseating, swapping, restarting, and checking accessories, verify whether the missing parameter returned and remained stable. If the module repeatedly drops out or the parameter disappears again, treat the issue as unresolved. Expected outcome: The monitor either passes functional verification or is removed from service.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported that the Spacelabs Xprezzon monitor would not recognize the parameter module and the SpO2 parameter was missing from the display.
CCR.Cause: Module was reseated and accessories were swapped, but the same module failed to be recognized on multiple compatible monitors.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the suspect module from service, labeled it Out of Service, and sent it for bench evaluation while the monitor was returned to use with a known-good module.

helpfulDetails: Whether a known-good module was tested
Whether the suspect module worked on another monitor
Evidence of fluid, impact, heat, or connector damage

title: Spacelabs Healthcare Xprezzon Patient Monitor - Network / central station communication failure
description: Troubleshooting Xprezzon monitors not communicating with the central station, network, remote viewing, or alarm display systems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the Xprezzon is actively monitoring a patient and communication to the central station is lost, notify clinical staff immediately. Do not assume central station alarm visibility is active until confirmed. Move the patient to another monitored bed, use a known-good monitor, or provide direct bedside observation according to clinical policy. Expected outcome: Patient monitoring and alarm awareness are maintained without relying on failed network communication. Why it matters: Loss of network connectivity may prevent alarms and patient data from appearing at central stations or other networked displays. Spacelabs documentation identifies Xprezzon as a network-capable bedside monitor, and FDA recall documentation has described loss of network connectivity as affecting central station alarm and data display.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Communication Failure
steps.instructions: Check whether the issue is: No patient data at the central station; Bedside monitor missing from the central station; Delayed or frozen waveform display; Alarm not appearing centrally; Remote view unavailable; Monitor visible locally but not over the network. Confirm whether bedside monitoring functions still display correctly on the Xprezzon. Expected outcome: The problem is identified as a network or central station communication issue, not a general monitor failure.

steps.title: Check Whether the Issue Is Isolated or Widespread
steps.instructions: Ask staff whether other Spacelabs monitors on the same unit are communicating normally. Check nearby monitors connected to the same network area or central station. Expected outcome: One failed monitor suggests a local cable, port, configuration, or monitor issue. Multiple failed monitors suggest a network switch, central station, server, or IT infrastructure issue. If multiple monitors are affected, escalate to Clinical Engineering leadership and IT/network support before replacing parts.

steps.title: Verify Bedside Monitor Power and Normal Operation
steps.instructions: Confirm the Xprezzon is powered on, fully booted, and not frozen. Check for normal waveform and numeric display at the bedside. Confirm there are no obvious system errors, lockups, repeated restarts, or abnormal boot behavior. Expected outcome: The monitor is operating locally before network communication is investigated. If the monitor is frozen or repeatedly rebooting, remove it from service and send for bench evaluation.

steps.title: Check Network Cable Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect the network cable at the monitor and wall jack. Confirm the cable is fully seated and not loose, bent, crushed, pulled tight, or routed where bed movement may strain it. Reseat both ends of the cable if accessible and safe. Expected outcome: A loose or partially disconnected network cable is corrected. If communication restores, verify central station visibility and stop.

steps.title: Inspect Network Link Indicators
steps.instructions: Check the network port on the monitor, dock, wall jack, or associated network adapter if visible. Look for link/activity lights. Expected outcome: Link lights should indicate an active physical network connection. If no link light is present, continue checking the cable, wall port, and network pathway.

steps.title: Swap with a Known-Good Network Cable
steps.instructions: Replace the current network cable with a known-good hospital-approved cable. Avoid using damaged, unlabeled, excessively long, or non-approved cables. Expected outcome: Communication returns if the original cable was defective. If communication restores, document the failed cable and remove it from use. Stop.

steps.title: Test the Wall Network Jack
steps.instructions: Move the monitor to a known-good network jack assigned for patient monitoring, or connect a known-good monitor to the suspect jack if appropriate. Do not connect to random wall ports unless the network assignment is verified. Expected outcome: The issue follows either the wall jack/network drop or the monitor. If the suspect jack fails with multiple monitors, escalate to IT/network support. If the Xprezzon fails on a known-good jack, continue troubleshooting the monitor-side issue.

steps.title: Confirm Correct Network Location and Bed Assignment
steps.instructions: Verify the monitor is being used in the correct room, bed, care area, or network zone. Confirm the central station is looking for the correct bed label or monitor assignment. Check whether the monitor was recently moved, swapped, replaced, or returned from repair. Expected outcome: The monitor appears under the correct bed or central station location. Why it matters: A monitor may be working normally but assigned to a different bed, sector, or central station view.

steps.title: Check Patient Admit / Discharge / Monitor Association
steps.instructions: Confirm whether the patient has been admitted, discharged, transferred, or reassigned at the monitor or central station. Ask clinical staff whether the issue began after a bed move, patient transfer, or monitor swap. Expected outcome: Patient and bed association is corrected if the monitor was not properly assigned. If communication restores after correct assignment, verify waveform and alarm visibility at the central station and stop.

steps.title: Review Central Station Display and Alarm Behavior
steps.instructions: At the central station, confirm whether the monitor is: Missing completely; Present but showing no waveforms; Showing old or frozen data; Displaying a communication or network message; Assigned to the wrong bed; Silenced, hidden, discharged, or removed from view. Expected outcome: The central station side of the failure is confirmed. If the issue is only at one central station display, troubleshoot that workstation or escalate appropriately.

steps.title: Restart Only When Clinically Safe
steps.instructions: If the patient is not dependent on the affected monitor, or monitoring has been safely transferred, restart the bedside monitor according to facility procedure. Do not reboot a monitor that is actively needed for continuous patient monitoring without clinical approval. Expected outcome: Temporary communication faults may clear after a controlled restart. If communication restores, confirm stable central station display and alarm visibility before returning to use.

steps.title: Check for Recent Configuration or Network Changes
steps.instructions: Ask whether any of the following occurred recently: Network maintenance; Central station restart; Monitor replacement; Room renovation; Wall jack repair; IP address, VLAN, or switch changes; Software update; Bedside monitor moved from another unit. Expected outcome: Recent changes help identify whether the failure is device-specific or infrastructure-related.

steps.title: Escalate for Network or System-Level Review
steps.instructions: If the cable, wall jack, bed assignment, and local monitor operation appear normal, escalate to the appropriate support group. This may include Clinical Engineering, IT/networking, Spacelabs system administration, or vendor support depending on site structure. Provide the affected room, bed, monitor serial number, network port, central station name, time of failure, and whether other beds are affected. Expected outcome: Network configuration, central station routing, switch port status, or system communication issues are reviewed by the proper support team.

steps.title: Remove from Service if the Monitor Itself Remains Suspect
steps.instructions: If the Xprezzon does not communicate on a known-good cable, known-good network jack, and known-good central station assignment, remove it from clinical use. Label it Out of Service and send it for bench evaluation. Expected outcome: A potentially faulty monitor network interface or internal communication problem is removed from patient care.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported that the Spacelabs Xprezzon in ICU Bed 12 was displaying patient vitals locally but was not appearing at the central station.
CCR.Cause: Found network cable loose at the wall jack with no active link light present.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated the network cable, verified link activity, confirmed waveform and alarm visibility at the central station, and returned the monitor to service.

helpfulDetails: Patient connected at time of failure
Other monitors affected or not affected
Recent monitor move or bed reassignment
